channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Netanyahu appoints advisor with Trump ties to lead ceasefire talks
item.description: An Israeli official said that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has appointed a close confidant to lead negotiations for the second stage of the ceasefire with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The pope slept peacefully, had breakfast after diagnosis of pneumonia in both lungs, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis slept well and ate breakfast Wednesday, the Vatican said, after he developed pneumonia in both lungs in a further complication in the condition of the 88-year-old pontiff that raised concerns about his ability to fight off the infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing user data with TikTok owner ByteDance
item.description: "We confirmed DeepSeek communicating with ByteDance," a South Korean data protection regulator told Yonhap News Agency.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil’s prosecutor-general files charges against ex-President Bolsonaro over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Prosecutor-General Paulo Gonet has filed charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro, alleging he attempted a coup to stay in office following his 2022 election loss.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan bill would rename street near Russian envoy's residence after Navalny
item.description: Senate Minority Whip Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) and Sen. Bill Cassidy (R-La.) on Tuesday introduced a bill proposing to rename part of the street near the Russian ambassador’s residence in Washington, D.C., as “Alexei Navalny Way.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Nearly 300 deportees from US held in Panama hotel as officials try to return them to their countries
item.description: Panama is detaining in a hotel nearly 300 people from various countries deported by the United States, not allowing them to leave while waiting for international authorities to organize a return to their countries.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Zelensky 'should have never started' war with Russia
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday appeared to blame Ukraine's leaders for the three-year war with Russia, arguing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "should have never started it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Rubio makes progress with Russia after Trump promised to bring end to war in Ukraine
item.description: Secretary of State Macro Rubio said first step in ending the war in Ukraine is reestablishing diplomacy with Russia by reopening embassies in Moscow and Washington, as EU watches with concern.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky delays trip to Saudi Arabia amid US-Russia talks
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has postponed an official visit to Saudi Arabia set for this week amid U.S.-Russia negotiations in Riyadh.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: A deeper look at the talks between US and Russian officials as Trump suggests Ukraine is to blame
item.description: Top U.S. and Russian officials have had their most extensive high-level engagement since Moscow sent troops into Ukraine almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Vladimir Isachenkov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: French bill would ban headscarves in sport; Amnesty International says it's discriminatory
item.description: Amnesty International is urging French lawmakers to reject a bill to ban religious clothing and symbols, including headscarves, during competitions, saying it is discriminatory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia frees detained American as talks with US on Ukraine begin
item.description: Russia has freed an American citizen detained on drug charges, in what the State Department called a "welcome gesture" ahead of a meeting between U.S. and Russian officials in Saudi Arabia. American Kalob Wayne Byers, 28, was released earlier this week to the custody of U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: North Korea vows to expand nuclear forces, blasts US for 'outdated' denuclearization plan
item.description: North Korea vowed to expand its nuclear forces under Kim Jong Un, criticizing the U.S. and its neighbors in Asia for pushing an "absurd" denuclearization plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over 4 bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: A senior Hamas leader says the militant group will release six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and the bodies of four others on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: KHIAM, Lebanon (AP) — Sabah Abdullah comes to her hometown in Lebanon every morning and sits next to her destroyed home. She is waiting for experts from Hezbollah's construction arm to compensate her for the damage caused by the Israel-Hezbollah war that has left her homeless.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iraqi activists seek to oust Iranian influence, normalize ties with Israel
item.description: Iraqi activists launched a campaign called "Iraq Only" to reject interference from Iran-aligned armed militias in their nation. Some of the organizers were kidnapped and tortured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many weary Ukrainians long for an end to the war but now fear it will come on unfavorable terms
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — After nearly three years of living under constant threat of Russian airstrikes while their troops fight a grinding campaign against Russia's invasion, many Ukrainians long for an end to the war — but now fear it could come on unfavorable terms. Top U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy travels to UAE as momentum grows for talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates on Monday as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Russia's war on the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 18 killed in stampede at New Delhi railway station in India as Hindu pilgrims travel to festival
item.description: In New Delhi, India, at least 18 people were killed in a stampede at a railway station. Many victims were Hindu pilgrims traveling to the Maha Kumbh festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump urged to confront Iranian regime over repression of Christians
item.description: Iran’s repression of Christians continues to grow, according to advocacy groups and analysts. Earlier this month the regime re-arrested two men who had previously been jailed due to their faith.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gold mine collapse kills 42 in Mali
item.description: Late on Saturday, Malian television announced the collapse of the site at Bilali Koto in the commune of Dabia, with a provisional death toll of 42 and many injured.
item.dc:creator: BABA AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
